message:
🌱 Add metallb: Existing services with multiple external IPs prevent setup mission (#819)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,81 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "metallb-1431-existing-services-with-more-than-two-external-ips-prevent-metallb-f",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "metallb: Existing services with more than two external IPs prevent MetalLB from completing setup",
+    "description": "Existing services with more than two external IPs prevent MetalLB from completing setup. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current metallb deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your MetalLB deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n metallb-system\nhelm list -n metallb-system 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis issue affects MetalLB deployments where pre-existing services have multiple external IPs."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review metallb configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant MetalLB configuration and services:\n```bash\nkubectl get svc -A -o wide | grep LoadBalancer\nkubectl get ipaddresspools.metallb.io -n metallb-system\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb -n metallb-system --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for services with multiple external IPs that may prevent MetalLB from completing setup. Check for error messages like `Failed to retrieve lbIPs family`."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Existing services with more than two external IPs prevent\u2026",
+        "description": "The fix handles services with malformed load balancer IPs gracefully. When MetalLB encounters a service with invalid LB IPs and cannot determine the IP family, instead of erroring out and reprocessing in a loop, it now clears the invalid status and lets the assignment logic add a new IP.\n\nExample MetalLB configuration:\n```yaml\napiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1\nkind: IPAddressPool\nmetadata:\n  name: default\n  namespace: metallb-system\nspec:\n  addresses:\n  - 192.168.1.180-192.168.1.189\n---\napiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1\nkind: L2Advertisement\nmetadata:\n  name: default\n  namespace: metallb-system\nspec:\n  ipAddressPools:\n  - default\n```\n\nSee the fix PR for details: https://github.com/metallb/metallb/pull/1778"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb\nkubectl get events -n metallb-system --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Existing services with more than two external IPs prevent\u2026\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Currently, when metallb receives a service with malformed lb ips and it's not able to determine the ip family, it errors out and reprocesses the service.\n\nHere if the lb part of the status is invalid we just wipe it and let the assignement logic add a new ip. This prevents malformed services to cause infinite loops and helps in recovering the service.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "NAMESPACE              NAME                              TYPE           CLUSTER-IP      EXTERNAL-IP                                 PORT(S)                      AGE\ndefault                kubernetes                        ClusterIP      10.43.0.1       <none>                                      443/TCP                      259d\nkube-system            kube-dns                          ClusterIP      10.43.0.10      <none>                                      53/UDP,53/TCP,9153/TCP       259d\nkube-system            metrics-server                    ClusterIP      10.43.52.43     <none>                                      443/TCP                      259d\nkube-system            traefik                           LoadBalancer   10.43.210.150   192.168.1.170,192.168.1.171,192.168.1.172   80:32",
+        "---\napiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1\nkind: IPAddressPool\nmetadata:\n  name: default\n  namespace: metallb-system\nspec:\n  addresses:\n  - 192.168.1.180-192.168.1.189\n\n---\napiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1\nkind: L2Advertisement\nmetadata:\n  name: default\n  namespace: metallb-system\nspec:\n  ipAddressPools:\n  - default",
+        "{\"caller\":\"service.go:74\",\"event\":\"clearAssignment\",\"level\":\"error\",\"msg\":\"Failed to retrieve lbIPs family\",\"reason\":\"nolbIPsIPFamily\",\"ts\":\"2022-11-28T20:18:02Z\"}"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "metallb",
+      "sandbox",
+      "app-definition",
+      "deploy"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "metallb"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Ingress",
+      "Node",
+      "Service"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "deploy"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/metallb/metallb/issues/1431",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/metallb/metallb",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/metallb/metallb/pull/1778"
+    },
+    "reactions": 4,
+    "comments": 19,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with metallb installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-12T06:37:51.247Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,

+  }
+}
